title: Mossel Bay finance chair in George Court today
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Mossel Bay Municipality's portfolio chairperson for Finance Alderman Mark Furness and a co-accused, Marie Francis, will make their fourth appearance in the George Magistrate's Court today, Wednesday 23 April.


> The charges against them are tax-related and involve the business, Bardins Jewellers. Furness and Francis have 75% and 25% interest in Bardins, respectively.


They are facing 14 counts of tax-related offences.

Hawks Western Cape spokesperson, Warrant Officer Zinzi Hani, confirmed this and said the charges against them include failure to appoint a representative taxpayer, failure to submit an income tax return and failure to submit value value-added tax return.

Previous articles:

- Finance chair to appear in court, charges unrelated to work, says mayor
- Finance chair, co-accused appear: 14 counts of tax offences
- Mossel Bay finance chair's court case postponed due to outstanding docket

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
